title: Multiline string literals should be avoided
description: Creating multiline strings by using a backslash (\) before a newline is known as "line continuation" or "line breaking." While it may seem like a convenient way to format multiline strings, it is generally considered bad practice. Line continuation can make the code harder to read and understand, especially when dealing with long strings. It introduces an extra character at the end of each line, which can clutter the code and reduce its readability.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Multiline string literals using backslash continuation should be avoided. Use template literals instead.
